s1_after_click.png.step_retrieval_summary: LibreOffice Impress, Normal view on slide 1. An image on the canvas is selected (green handles); the Properties sidebar is open to Image and Position & Size. Keep ratio is checked and rotation is 0°, ready for resizing or positioning.

s2_after_drag.png.step_retrieval_summary: LibreOffice Impress with slide 1 active. An image on the canvas is selected (resize handles visible). Properties sidebar is open to Image > Position and Size; X/Y, Width, and Height fields are editable and Keep ratio is checked.

s3_after_drag.png.step_retrieval_summary: LibreOffice Impress with Slide 1 active. An image on the slide is selected (resize handles visible), status shows “Resize Image.” Properties sidebar is open to Image > Position and Size; Width/Height fields are editable and Keep ratio is checked.

s4_after_click.png.step_retrieval_summary: LibreOffice Impress in Normal view with Slide 1 active. A large image on the slide is selected (resize handles visible). The Properties sidebar is open to Image > Position and Size; Keep ratio is checked and width/height/position and rotation/flip controls are ready for editing.

s5_after_click.png.step_retrieval_summary: LibreOffice Impress with slide 1 (Title Slide) active. An image object is selected on the slide. Properties sidebar is open to Image with Position and Size expanded; Position X/Y and Width/Height fields are editable, Keep ratio checked, and Rotation control visible.

s6_after_press.png.step_retrieval_summary: LibreOffice Impress is active with slide 1 displayed. An image on the slide is selected (“Image ‘Picture 2’ selected”). The Properties sidebar is open to Image > Position and Size; X/Y, Width and Height fields are editable, Keep ratio is checked, and Rotation is 0°.

init.png.step_retrieval_summary: LibreOffice Impress with slide 1 active on the canvas and selected in the thumbnails. The Properties sidebar is open on the Slide tab; the Layouts grid is shown with a title layout selected. Master Slide is set to “Title Slide” with Master Background and Master Objects enabled.
